name: Bull Strap
tagline: Massive catalog with competitive pricing on premium off-road parts
product_range: Seat covers, limit straps, grab handles, off-road accessories, Jeep parts
pros: Enormous product selection across multiple categories
cons: Newer brand — less name recognition than legacy retailers
Some niche categories still being built out
review: Bull Strap has quietly become one of the best online destinations for off-road and automotive parts. Their catalog is genuinely impressive — we're talking thousands of SKUs spanning everything from premium seat covers to suspension components to trail accessories. What sets them apart is the combination of breadth and pricing. You'll find products here that compete directly with the big-box auto parts sites, but with better curation and a cleaner shopping experience. Their Jeep parts section alone is worth bookmarking. If you're building a rig and want a one-stop shop that doesn't feel like a cluttered warehouse, Bull Strap delivers.

name: RockAuto
tagline: The no-frills parts warehouse with unbeatable prices
product_range: OEM replacement parts, maintenance parts, body panels, engine components
pros: Lowest prices on most OEM replacement parts
Massive inventory — virtually every make/model covered
review: RockAuto is the Costco of auto parts — no frills, no fancy website, just incredible prices on a staggering inventory. If you need a replacement alternator, brake pads, or oil filter, RockAuto will almost certainly have it cheaper than anyone else. The website is famously ugly but functionally excellent once you learn the navigation. Where RockAuto falls short is in the aftermarket and off-road space — this is a maintenance and repair parts destination, not where you go to outfit a trail rig. But for what it does, it's nearly unbeatable.

name: Summit Racing
tagline: The enthusiast's go-to for performance and off-road parts
shipping: Free shipping on orders over $109, fast fulfillment
product_range: Performance parts, off-road, racing, engine builds, suspension, lighting
pros: Legendary reputation in the enthusiast community
Expert tech support available by phone
Huge selection of performance and off-road parts
cons: Can be overwhelming for casual buyers
Shipping costs add up on heavy items
review: Summit Racing has been a pillar of the automotive aftermarket for decades, and their online store reflects that heritage. The selection is massive — from engine internals to off-road suspension kits to racing harnesses. What really distinguishes Summit is their tech support team. Call them with a question about fitment or compatibility and you'll talk to someone who actually knows what they're talking about. Prices aren't always the lowest, but the combination of selection, expertise, and reliability makes Summit a perennial top pick.

name: ExtremeTerrain
tagline: Jeep and truck specialists with deep fitment data
product_range: Jeep Wrangler/Gladiator parts, truck accessories, lift kits, bumpers, lighting
pros: Best-in-class fitment guarantee for Jeep parts
cons: Primarily Jeep/truck focused — limited for other vehicles
Some items drop-shipped with longer lead times
Prices slightly higher than wholesale sources
review: If you drive a Jeep or full-size truck, ExtremeTerrain should be in your bookmarks. Their vehicle-specific filtering is the best in the business — enter your year, model, and trim, and you'll only see parts that actually fit. The install guides and video content are genuinely helpful, especially for first-time modifiers. They carry a solid range of brands including premium seat covers from manufacturers like Bartact. Pricing is competitive though not always the cheapest — you're paying a bit for the fitment guarantee and curated experience.

name: Quadratec
tagline: The original Jeep parts superstore — 30+ years deep
product_range: Jeep parts exclusively — tops, bumpers, armor, lighting, interior, recovery
pros: Exclusive house-brand products at good value
cons: Jeep-only — if you drive anything else, look elsewhere
House brand quality varies by category
Website can be slow during sales
review: Quadratec is the OG Jeep parts retailer. They've been at this for over three decades and it shows in their depth of knowledge and product curation. Their house-brand products (like tops and covers) offer solid value, and they carry all the major aftermarket brands. The tech articles section is a goldmine if you're researching a build. For Jeep owners, Quadratec is essentially a one-stop shop that combines the selection of a big retailer with the expertise of a specialty shop.

name: 4 Wheel Parts
tagline: Brick-and-mortar meets online with nationwide install centers
shipping: Free shipping over $99, in-store pickup available
product_range: Lift kits, tires/wheels, bumpers, winches, lighting, recovery gear
review: 4 Wheel Parts bridges the gap between online shopping and hands-on service. With over 100 locations across the U.S., they offer something most online retailers can't: professional installation. Buy a lift kit online, schedule an install at your local store, and show up with coffee. That convenience has real value. Their online prices tend to run a bit higher than pure e-commerce competitors, but the install option and ability to see/touch products in person makes up for it if you have a location nearby.

name: Amazon Auto Parts
tagline: The everything store — auto parts included
shipping: Prime 1-2 day shipping on eligible items
product_range: Everything — OEM, aftermarket, accessories, tools, fluids
pros: Customer reviews help with fitment decisions
cons: Customer service knows nothing about cars
review: Amazon needs no introduction, and their automotive section is exactly what you'd expect — massive selection, Prime shipping, and the occasional minefield of questionable sellers. For basics like filters, fluids, and universal accessories, Amazon is tough to beat on convenience. But for specific aftermarket parts, we'd recommend going to a specialist retailer where fitment data is reliable and support staff actually understand what you're buying. The garage feature helps with fitment, but it's not foolproof.

name: AutoZone
tagline: The maintenance and repair stalwart with same-day availability
shipping: Free next-day delivery or same-day store pickup
product_range: Maintenance parts, fluids, batteries, tools, basic accessories
pros: Duralast house brand is solid value
cons: Online prices sometimes higher than competitors
Not an off-road or enthusiast destination
review: AutoZone is where you go when your check engine light comes on and you need parts today. With over 6,000 locations, same-day pickup is almost always an option. Their Duralast house brand offers good value on batteries, brake components, and other wear items. The free services (battery testing, code reading, tool loans) add genuine value. But let's be clear — AutoZone is a maintenance parts store, not an enthusiast destination. You won't find lift kits or trail armor here.

name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
tagline: Professional-grade parts availability with retail accessibility
product_range: Maintenance, repair, professional-grade parts, tools, accessories
pros: Professional-grade parts available to retail customers
Better trained staff than most retail chains
Machine shop services at many locations
cons: Prices comparable to AutoZone — not the cheapest
Website is functional but not inspiring
review: O'Reilly occupies a sweet spot between retail auto parts stores and professional suppliers. Their parts quality tends to skew slightly higher than AutoZone, and their staff — particularly at stores near trade schools or in gearhead towns — can be surprisingly knowledgeable. The machine shop services (rotor turning, drum grinding) at select locations are a nice bonus. Like AutoZone, this isn't where you shop for trail gear, but for maintenance and repair parts, O'Reilly is a reliable choice.

name: Advance Auto Parts
tagline: Solid parts retailer with a strong online presence
shipping: Free shipping on orders over $35, store pickup
pros: SpeedPerks loyalty program has real value
review: Advance Auto Parts has been working hard to modernize, and their online experience shows it. The $35 free shipping threshold is the lowest among major chains, and the SpeedPerks loyalty program actually returns meaningful value if you're a regular buyer. The acquisition of the DieHard battery brand was smart. In-store experience remains hit-or-miss depending on location, but for online ordering with store pickup, Advance is perfectly solid.

name: eBay Motors
tagline: The marketplace for rare, used, and hard-to-find parts
product_range: New, used, refurbished — every category imaginable
cons: Fitment accuracy depends on seller knowledge
review: eBay Motors is the wild west of auto parts — and sometimes that's exactly what you need. Looking for a discontinued OEM part? A used transfer case for your '90s rig? A take-off bumper from someone else's build? eBay is where those things live. The buyer protection has improved significantly over the years, making it less risky than it used to be. Just do your due diligence on seller ratings and don't expect the curated experience you'd get from a specialty retailer.

name: Turn 14 Distribution
tagline: Wholesale powerhouse for dealers and serious builders
cons: Dealer account required — not open to retail public
review: Turn 14 Distribution isn't a store you shop at — it's where the stores you shop at get their inventory. As one of the largest performance and off-road parts distributors in the U.S., Turn 14 supplies thousands of retailers and shops with products from over 1,500 brands. If you're a shop owner, dealer, or serious builder who buys in volume, a Turn 14 account gives you access to wholesale pricing and some of the best logistics in the industry. For everyone else, you're buying Turn 14 inventory through retailers like Bull Strap and others on this list — you just don't know it.
